input.background: Let us return to the birth weight illustrative data concerning 10 SIDS cases. We have established that \bar{x}=2890.5 grams and s=720.0.
input.question: Confidence intervals for \mu at 99 \% level of confidence will be calculated.

output.answer: For 99 \% confidence, use t_{9, .995}=3.250, so the 99 \% confidence interval for \mu is 2890.5 \pm(3.250)(227.7)=2890.5 \pm 740.0= (2150.5 to 3630.5) grams.

mean_birth_weight.description: The mean birth weight of the sample, used to calculate the confidence interval.

standard_deviation.description: The standard deviation of the birth weight in the sample, used to calculate the confidence interval.

confidence_level.description: The level of confidence for the interval estimation, indicating the probability that the interval contains the true mean.

sample_size.description: The number of SIDS cases in the sample, used in the calculation of the confidence interval.
